resetSettings.tooltip: Click to reset all of your {{name}} to their default.
resetSettings.confirmHtml: Are you sure you want to reset your {{name}} to the default? This action is irreversible!
resetSettings.successHtml: {{name}} are now set to the default. You may have to reload the app to reflect certain changes.

tooltips.lbl_languageSelect: Set the default language for PianoRhythm.

elements.lbl_resetSettingsModalBodyText:
Are you sure you want to do this?

This will reset all your settings to the default and cannot be undone.

tooltips.tgl_checkForUpdates: Whether the application should check for updates periodically.
tooltips.tgl_enableDebugMode: Puts PianoRhythm in a kind of debug mode. Logging should be a bit more verbose and certain features should/will be more debug friendly.
tooltips.btn_openCookieManagement: Click to manage your cookie preferences.
tooltips.btn_exportLogFile: Click to choose a location for PianoRhythm's log file.
tooltips.btn_openAppDataFolder: Click to open PianoRhythm's App Data folder.

messages.noLogFile: Sorry, there's no log file to export at the moment. Please try again later.
messages.logExportSuccess: Log file has been exported successfully.
messages.logExportError: Something went wrong trying to export the log file. Please check the console logs for more details.
messages.appDataFolderError: Something went wrong trying to open the App Data folder. Please check the console logs for more details.
messages.resetAllConfirmText: Are you sure you want to reset your settings to the default? This action is irreversible!
messages.resetAllSuccessText: Settings are now set to the default. You may have to reload the app to reflect certain changes.

elements.txt_passwordChangeSubText: Enter your current password and the new password
elements.txt_deleteAccountSubText: Are you really sure? All of your account data will be permanently deleted
elements.txt_onIncorrectDeleteInputField: Please make sure you typed correctly in the input field.
elements.txt_confirmPasswordSubText: Please enter and confirm your password
elements.txt_emailChangeSubText: Enter the new email address and your password

tooltips.tgl_enable2fa: Add an extra layer of security with two-factor authentication (2FA).
tooltips.tgl_allowUsersToNotifyMe: Toggle whether you get notifications when users @ a.k.a notify you, in a message.
tooltips.btn_deleteAccount: This will trigger the process to permanently delete your PianoRhythm account.
tooltips.clickToUploadProfileImage: Click to upload a profile image!

messages.on2faDisable: After disabling two-factor authentication, logging in to your account will require password-only protection. Do you wish to continue with disabling two-factor authentication?
messages.deleteAccountSuccess:
You've successfully deleted your account!

After this, your current session will end and you'll be directed to the login screen.
messages.requestError: Something went wrong with the request.
messages.deleteAccountIntro:
We're sorry to see you go!


Please note that this process is final and there'll be no way to restore your account.


Any active subscriptions will also be canceled.
messages.bothFieldsValid: Please make sure both fields are valid.

tooltips.hdr_notifications: Toggle what server notifications you receive.
tooltips.tgl_showMyCursor: Toggle whether your cursor is displayed to others.
tooltips.tgl_sendWhoistyping: Toggle whether other users know when you're typing.
tooltips.tgl_disableWhispers: Disable whispering. You can't whisper to others and vice versa.
tooltips.tgl_friendsOnlineStatus: Get notified when a friend comes online
tooltips.tgl_friendsOfflineStatus: Get notified when a friend goes offline
tooltips.tgl_emitMyStatusToFriends: Allow friends to know of your status changes. (Online, Offline, etc)
tooltips.tgl_sheetMusicChanges: Get notification emails whenever a change happens to your uploaded sheet music.

tooltips.swtch_syncDiscordMessages: Toggle whether Discord messages are synced in main lobby.

elements.msg_bindingConflict: There's already a command that shares the same binding
elements.msg_resetLayoutConfirm: Are you sure you want to reset the layout to what you had initially?
elements.msg_clearMappingConfirm: Are you sure you want to clear the current mapping?
elements.msg_octaveIncreased: Mapped Keys Notes have been increased by one octave!
elements.msg_octaveDecreased: Mapped Keys Notes have been decreased by one octave!
elements.tgl_useCapslockForMidiQwerty: Use capslock instead of ctrl for MIDI to QWERTY Mod

tooltips.lbl_pianoKeylayout: Set the keyboard piano layout for the piano.
tooltips.lbl_keyboardMaplayout: Set the correct keyboard input mapping.
tooltips.tgl_keyboard_toggleyz: For certain keyboard layouts, switching the mapping of the Y and Z keyboards aligns more naturally.
tooltips.tgl_ctrlKeyLowersOctave:
Pressing the Ctrl key will lower the octave of the notes played.

Note: This feature is disabled when using the midi to qwerty converter layout for VP.
tooltips.tgl_midiToVpQwertyLayout:
Use a similar extended layout compared the midi to VP/qwerty converter tool.
Link to: MIDI to Virtual Piano Converter
tooltips.tgl_useCapslockForMidiQwerty: This is to provide an alternative key because certain shortcuts in the browser may conflict with the default ctrl key binding. For example, ctrl + w closes the current tab in most browsers.

elements.tgl_enableAutoAnimateCamera: Enable Auto Animate Camera To Instruments

tooltips.lbl_engine: Toggle between 2D, 3D, or no graphics engine.
tooltips.lbl_maxFPS: Select the max frame rate of the rendering engine.
tooltips.lbl_graphicsPreset:
Quickly switch between curated graphics quality presets. Choosing a preset will toggle multiple graphics options at once.

Selecting "Custom" indicates the current settings don't match any built-in preset.
tooltips.lbl_graphicsRenderer:
Select the graphics rendering engine.

The "Bevy" renderer is currently very experimental and may not have the same features as the "Babylon.js" renderer.

NOTE: Requires a reload to take effect.
tooltips.lbl_targetFPS:
Select the target FPS for the rendering engine.

NOTE: Requires a reload to take effect.
tooltips.lbl_mirrorTextureSampleSize:
The mirror texture sample size. The larger the size, the better the quality of the reflection.

NOTE: Requires a reload to take effect.
tooltips.lbl_msaaSamples:
Multisample anti-aliasing samples per pixel. Higher values produce smoother edges at the cost of GPU performance and memory bandwidth.

2x and 8x may not be supported on all devices; 4x is the safest choice on the web.
tooltips.lbl_webglPowerPreference:
Provides a hint to WebGL regarding the preferred power preference mode.
  • When set to Default, the browser decides which GPU configuration is most suitable.
  • When set to High Performance, a GPU configuration that prioritizes rendering performance over power consumption is selected.
  • When set to Low Power, a GPU configuration that prioritizes power saving over rendering performance is selected.
NOTE: Requires a reload to take effect.
tooltips.lbl_enginePerformancePriority:
Determines how the graphics scene will treat performance regarding backward compatibility and ease of use.
  • When set to BackwardCompatible, there is simply no change. The scene will keep prioritizing ease of use and backward compatibility.
  • When set to Intermediate, some performance options will be turned on trying to strike a balance between perf and ease of use.
  • When set to Aggressive, performance will be top priority.
NOTE: Requires a reload to take effect.

Source: Performance Priority Modes
tooltips.tgl_enableGraphicsEngine:
Toggle whether the graphics engine is initialized and used for rendering.

NOTE: Requires a reload to take effect.
tooltips.tgl_enableStage: Enable/disable the main stage in the scene.
tooltips.tgl_enablePiano: Enable/disable showing the main piano in the scene.
tooltips.tgl_enablePianoBench: Enable/disable showing the piano bench in the scene.
tooltips.tgl_enableDrums: Enable/disable showing the drum set (only will show when there's activity in the drum channel) in the scene.
tooltips.tgl_enableMotionBlur: Motion blur is an effect used primarily in games where the surroundings appear blurry on the screen as the camera moves quickly through a particular area.
tooltips.tgl_enableAntialiasing:
Defines whether MSAA (Multisample anti-aliasing) is enabled on the canvas. This is a technique used in computer graphics to remove jagged edges. Disabling this may improve performance.

NOTE: Requires a reload to take effect.
tooltips.tgl_enableParticleSystem:
A particle system is a collection of many many minute particles that together represent a fuzzy object.

NOTE: When disabled, the particles won't immediately dissipate until their life time is complete.
tooltips.tgl_enableFog: Distance fog is a technique used in 3D computer graphics to enhance the perception of distance by shading distant objects differently.
tooltips.tgl_enablePostProcessing: Enable/disable post processing effects in the scene.
tooltips.tgl_enableGlow: Enable/disable glow effects on certain models.
tooltips.tgl_enablePianoKeysGlow: Adds a soft glow halo to the piano keys, independent of the main glow setting above. Useful for highlighting the keys without applying glow to the rest of the scene.
tooltips.tgl_enableSoftShadows: Enable/disable soft shadows in the scene.
tooltips.tgl_enableSpecialEffects: Enable/disable special effects (like rain) in the scene.
tooltips.tgl_enableAutoAnimateCamera: If enabled, the camera will animate and lock target with the instruments (camera, drums, etc) when you get close enough within range of them.
tooltips.tgl_enableOrchestraModels: Enable/disable other user models being shown in Orchestra mode.
tooltips.tgl_enableHDR: Enable High Dynamic Range rendering. Allows brighter highlights and richer colors beyond the standard [0,1] range, producing more realistic lighting at the cost of some performance.
tooltips.tgl_enableBloom: Adds a soft glow around bright areas of the scene to simulate the way real cameras and eyes respond to intense light. Looks best with HDR enabled.
tooltips.tgl_enableToneMapping: Maps high-dynamic-range color values into the displayable range with a filmic curve, preserving highlight and shadow detail. Recommended when HDR is enabled.
tooltips.tgl_enableDepthOfField: This is a predefined chain of postprocesses that helps achieving photograph-like realism.
tooltips.tgl_enableAmbientOcclusion:
This is a rendering pipeline (chained post-processes) that will compute the ambient occlusion of a given scene from the screen space.

Technical definition: Ambient occlusion is a computer graphics technique that simulates how light falls on an object. It's a global lighting method that adds realism to models by controlling the attenuation of ambient light due to occluded areas.
tooltips.tgl_enableMirrorTextures:
Mirror textures can be used to simulate the view from a mirror in a scene. It will dynamically be rendered every frame to adapt to the camera point of view. In this case, it used to show a reflection of the models, such as the piano, on the ground or other surfaces.

NOTE: Requires a reload to take effect.
tooltips.tgl_useWebGPU:
WebGPU is a new API for the web, which exposes modern hardware capabilities and allows rendering and computation operations on a GPU, similar to Direct3D 12, Metal, and Vulkan. Unlike the WebGL family of APIs, WebGPU offers access to more advanced GPU features and provides first-class support for general computations on the GPU.

The promise behind WebGPU is an exceptionally faster API provided through lower level control to system graphics resources from JavaScript. (Still experimental and some features may not work completely. May also not be suppported by many devices.)

NOTE: Requires a reload to take effect.
tooltips.tgl_webgpuSnapshot:
Snapshot rendering is a new rendering mode available starting with Babylon.js v5.0. Only WebGPU supports it: activating this mode in WebGL has no effect.

Note that the performance improvement is on the javascript side only: the GPU perf will be more or less the same with or without SR enabled.

The perf improvements can be quite large, especially when using the fast SR mode.

Source: WebGPU Snapshot Rendering
tooltips.tgl_webgpuNonCompatMode:
WebGPU can be run in a non compatibility mode (NCM - also called fast path) that can allow for better performances, depending on your scene.

The WebGPUEngine.compatibilityMode property is a switch which is true by default. In this mode, the WebGPU engine is in a full compatibility mode (CM) with WebGL, meaning all scenes that can be rendered with WebGL can also be rendered by WebGPU without any changes on your side.

When setting this property to false you switch to the non compatibility mode (NCM), allowing the engine to use another code path which may improve performances at the cost of some possible re-organisation of your code.

Source: WebGPU Non Compatibility Mode
tooltips.tgl_enableOfflineStorage: Indicate that you want to cache some of the 3D scene resources associated with PianoRhythm inside the local IndexedDB of the browser. It can enhance the gamer experience as the JSON and textures files can be directly loaded from the database rather than from the web.
tooltips.tgl_renderInBackground:
Gets or sets a boolean indicating if the engine must keep rendering, even if the window is not in foreground.

(Mostly applies when not using the offscreen canvas)
tooltips.tgl_useOffscreenCanvas:
"The OffscreenCanvas interface provides a canvas that can be rendered off screen, decoupling the DOM and the Canvas API so that the canvas element is no longer entirely dependent on the DOM."

(More of a performance improvement)

NOTE: Requires a reload to take effect.

tooltips.tgl_enable3dAvatars:
Toggle whether 3D avatars should show up in the scene.

NOTE: Requires a reload to take effect.
tooltips.tgl_enablePhysicsEngine:
Toggle the game world's physics engine.

NOTE: Requires a reload to take effect.

tooltips.hdr_displays: Toggle the displays of certain UI elements
tooltips.tgl_whoistyping: Toggle displaying when other users' are typing
tooltips.hdr_chat: Related UI settings for chat messages
tooltips.sld_chatMsgsHeightOffset: Set the height offset of the chatmessages container
tooltips.tgl_display3DStats: Toggle displaying extra stats/info about the 3d scene
tooltips.tgle_keepChatInFocus: Toggle whether to keep the chat in focus after submitting a message
tooltips.sld_chatMsg2DOpacity: Set the default opacity of the chat messages when it's unfocused. (2D mode)
tooltips.sld_chatMsg3DOpacity: Set the default opacity of the chat messages when it's unfocused. (3D mode)
tooltips.tgle_showEmbeddedLinks: Toggle whether links show embedded objects such as images or videos of the associated link
tooltips.tgl_autoHideBottomBar: Toggle whether the menu bottom bar auto hides, once the cursor leaves the area
tooltips.tgl_displaySideWidgetButtons: Display the side widget buttons (on the right). A lot of common actions (locking camera, stage effects, etc) will appear here.
tooltips.tgl_displayFps: Display the FPS (frames per second) counter of the scene.
tooltips.tgl_displayRenderStats: Display rendering statistics about the current scene.
tooltips.tgl_autoScrollChat: Always auto scroll to the bottom for new messages
tooltips.tgl_enableUrlPreview: Enable showing url previews using meta info from the site.
tooltips.tgl_disableChatMarkdown:
Disable Markdown (text formatting/markup) in chat messages.

Note: Takes effect on next restart.
tooltips.tgl_enableDesktopNotifications:
Toggle whether PianoRhythm can emit desktop notifications to you in your system tray.

Note: You must have Notifications enabled in your browser in order for it to work.
tooltips.tgl_enableUserNoteActivities: Enable seeing any note activities being reflected (like the user icon going up and down) on the user item in the sidebar.
tooltips.tgl_warnExternalLinks: If enabled, a popup will be displayed about warning you about opening external links in chat.
tooltips.tgl_enableSynthNoteActivities: Enable seeing any note activities being reflected in certain UI components like the channels flashing in the instrument dock.

tooltips.hdr_displays: Toggle the displays of certain blob related elements.

messages.fileTooLarge:
File is too large! Max 2GB.

File: {{name}}
messages.noResults:
Something went wrong! No results were found.

File: {{name}}
messages.loadAborted:
Soundfont file load was aborted!

File: {{name}}
messages.clearCacheConfirmText: Are you sure you want to clear the soundfonts that have been cached? This action is irreversible.
messages.clearCacheErrorHtml:
Something went wrong trying to clear the Soundfonts cache!

Please check the console error logs for more info.

elements.opt_interpLinear: A bit slower, reasonable audio quality.
elements.opt_interpFourthOrder: Requires 50% of the whole DSP processing time, good quality (default)
elements.opt_interpSeventhOrder: Consumes more processing time but has highest quality.

tooltips.lbl_audioEngine:
Select the default audio engine.
(Takes effect on next restart)
tooltips.tgl_enableDrums: When enabled, this will allow percussion sounds to be played on channel 9 (zero-index base)
tooltips.hdr_v3Audio: Settings specific to the V3 Audio Engine
tooltips.lbl_initalBuffersize: Explanation coming soon. (Takes effect on next restart)
tooltips.tgl_showDebuglogs: When enabled, this will output performance logs to the console
tooltips.tgl_useAudioWorklet:
If enabled, the audio engine will use the AudioWorklet API for audio processing. This introduces some slight input latency but should provide better audio quality and stability.

NOTE: Requires a reload to take effect.
tooltips.btn_loadCustomSoundfont: Click to load your own soundfont located on your machine.
tooltips.btn_loadDefaultSoundfont: Click to load the default soundfont.
tooltips.lbl_audioSynthesizer:
The target audio synthesizer. If a soundfont doesn't sound proper, here's where you can try switching to a different synthesizer.

Certain synthesizers may be experimental or not be fully featured. Use at your own discretion.

NOTE: Requires a reload to take effect.
tooltips.lbl_audioSampleRate:
By default (AUTO), the AudioContext will pick a sample rate that matches the underlying device.

In developing an audio sound for computers or telecommunication, the sample rate is the number of samples of a sound that are taken per second to represent the event digitally. The more samples taken per second, the more accurate the digital representation of the sound can be.

NOTE: Requires a reload to take effect.
tooltips.sld_maxPolyphony: Determines the max amount of distinct notes the audio engine can play at one time.
tooltips.sld_maxNoteOnTime: Determines the max amount of time (in seconds) that a sound can last, while a key is pressed.
tooltips.tgl_enableReverb: Enable audio reverb. Reverb is the persistence of sound after a sound is produced.
tooltips.tgl_cacheSoundfonts: Determines whether soundfonts loaded from PianoRhythm's cloud should be saved locally on your machine. This should help reduce load times.
tooltips.btn_clearSoundfontsCache: This will clear your cache. If you're on the web client, it will clear up the soundfonts stored in IndexedDB. If you're on desktop, it will clear the cached soundfonts saved in the app's data folder.
tooltips.lbl_interpolationMethod: Synthesis interpolation method. If you are experiencing any weird issues with certain soundfonts, messing around with this setting may help.
tooltips.lbl_audioDevices:
The target audio device.

NOTE: Requires a reload to take effect.

tooltips.tgl_enableBgMusic: If enabled, background piano music will play during certain screens.

tooltips.tgl_enableSoundEffects: If enabled, sound effects will be emitted throughout certain UI elements.
tooltips.tgl_enableChatSoundEffects: If enabled, sound effects will be emitted for related chat UI elements.
tooltips.sld_stageEffectsVolume: Determines the global volume for stage sound effects.

elements.tgl_outputOwnNotes: Output own notes to MIDI Outputs

tooltips.lbl_enableAudioToOutput: If checked and any output devices are active, then audio will only be emitted to the output device and not within PianoRhythm's audio engine
tooltips.tgl_mousePosVelocity:
This will enable having the mouse position determine the velocity of your computer keyboard input.

The closer your mouse is to the bottom of the browser window, the louder your notes will be
tooltips.sld_sustainCutoff: Set the release cutoff point for sustained notes. (Higher percentage = shorter sustain)
tooltips.tgl_enableVelocity: If unchecked, the raw velocity values from the source will not be used. Instead it'll be set to the max value (127)
tooltips.sld_velocityMuting: Mute notes with a velocity that is less than or equal to the meter value.
tooltips.sld_velocityBoost: Adds additional value to raw velocity
tooltips.lbl_metronome: Select the audio sample type to use for the metronome
tooltips.tgl_enableV2Reverb: Toggle whether reverb is enabled for the V2 audio engine
tooltips.tgl_listenToProgramChanges: Toggle whether PianoRhythm should listen to midi program change events and update the instrument dock, accordingly.
tooltips.tgl_autoFillEmptyChannels: Toggle whether empty channels in the instrument dock should be autofilled with the default instrument when a note on event occurs.
tooltips.tgl_useDefaultBankWhenMissing: Toggle whether to use the default bank (usually bank 0) when a soundfont does not have a particular bank.
tooltips.tgl_enableStereoPanning:
Toggle whether the audio engine supports panning the audio stream left or right.

So, the sound position of the notes will be relative to their position on the piano.

For example, notes closer to the right will be heard more so on the right speaker. Same goes for the left and those near the middle will be distributed across both speakers.
tooltips.sld_maxVelocity: Determines the max velocity of your midi notes.
tooltips.sld_minVelocity: Determines the minimum velocity of your midi notes.
tooltips.sld_maxMultiModeChannels: Determines the max amount channels to enable during multi mode.
tooltips.tgl_outputOwnNotes: If enabled, your own notes will be emitted to your MIDI Output devices.
tooltips.sld_globalUserVelocityPct: This controls the global total percentage of the raw velocity values that users emit onto your client.

tooltips.lbl_languageSelect: Set the default language for PianoRhythm.
